docs.title: GraphQL Overview
docs.text: Learn about GraphQL, its advantages, and when to use it over REST APIs.

docs.title: What is GraphQL?
docs.text: GraphQL is a query language and runtime for APIs, developed by Facebook in 2012 and open-sourced in 2015. Unlike REST, which exposes multiple endpoints for different resources, GraphQL provides a single endpoint where clients can request exactly the data they need.

docs.title: Key Characteristics
docs.text:
• Single Endpoint: All operations go through one URL (e.g., /graphql)
  • Strongly Typed: Schema defines all types, fields, and operations
  • Client-Driven: Clients specify exactly what data they need
  • Real-time: Built-in support for subscriptions
  • Introspectable: Schema can be queried for documentation

docs.title: GraphQL vs REST
docs.text: Aspect REST GraphQL Endpoints Multiple (/users, /posts, /comments) Single (/graphql) Data Fetching Fixed response structure Client specifies fields Over-fetching Common (get all fields) Eliminated (request specific fields) Under-fetching Requires multiple requests Single request for nested data Versioning URL versioning (/v1/, /v2/) Schema evolution (deprecation) HTTP Methods GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH POST (typically) Caching Built-in HTTP caching Custom implementation needed Learning Curve Lower Higher

docs.title: Example Comparison
docs.text:
REST Approach (3 requests): 

# Get user
GET /api/users/123

# Get user's posts
GET /api/users/123/posts

# Get comments for each post
GET /api/posts/456/comments

GraphQL Approach (1 request): 

query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    email
    posts(limit: 10) {
      title
      comments(limit: 5) {
        author
        text
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
The schema is the contract between client and server, defining:

# Types
type User {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  email: String
  posts: [Post!]!
}

type Post {
  id: ID!
  title: String!
  content: String
  author: User!
}

# Queries (read operations)
type Query {
  user(id: ID!): User
  users(limit: Int): [User!]!
  post(id: ID!): Post
}

# Mutations (write operations)
type Mutation {
  createUser(name: String!, email: String!): User!
  updateUser(id: ID!, name: String): User
  deleteUser(id: ID!): Boolean
}

# Subscriptions (real-time updates)
type Subscription {
  userCreated: User!
  postUpdated(userId: ID!): Post!
}

docs.title: Operations
docs.text:
GraphQL has three operation types:

  1. Query - Read data (like GET)
  2. Mutation - Modify data (like POST/PUT/DELETE)
  3. Subscription - Real-time updates (WebSocket)

docs.title: Fields
docs.text:
Fields are the units of data you request:

{
  user(id: "123") {
    name        # Scalar field
    email       # Nullable field
    posts {     # Object field (relationship)
      title
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
Fields can accept arguments:

{
  users(
    limit: 10
    offset: 20
    orderBy: "createdAt"
    filter: { role: "admin" }
  ) {
    name
  }
}

docs.title: Aliases
docs.text:
Rename fields in the response:

{
  smallPosts: posts(limit: 3) {
    title
  }
  largePosts: posts(limit: 100) {
    title
  }
}

docs.title: Fragments
docs.text:
Reusable groups of fields:

fragment UserFields on User {
  id
  name
  email
}

{
  user1: user(id: "1") {
    ...UserFields
  }
  user2: user(id: "2") {
    ...UserFields
  }
}

docs.title: Variables
docs.text:
Parameterize queries:

query GetUser($userId: ID!, $postLimit: Int = 10) {
  user(id: $userId) {
    name
    posts(limit: $postLimit) {
      title
    }
  }
}

Variables (JSON): 

{
  "userId": "123",
  "postLimit": 5
}

docs.title: ✅ Good Use Cases
docs.text:
1. Mobile Applications - Limited bandwidth - Battery efficiency - Need precise data fetching

2. Complex Data Requirements - Nested relationships (users → posts → comments) - Multiple related resources - Different views need different data

3. Rapid Frontend Development - Frontend teams control data needs - No backend changes for new UI requirements - Faster iteration cycles

4. Microservices Architecture - GraphQL gateway over multiple services - Single API layer - Unified schema

5. Real-time Applications - Chat applications - Live dashboards - Collaborative editing

docs.title: ❌ When to Avoid GraphQL
docs.text:
1. Simple CRUD APIs - Standard REST is sufficient - No complex relationships

2. File Uploads/Downloads - REST is better suited - HTTP caching benefits

3. Heavy Caching Requirements - REST HTTP caching is mature - GraphQL caching more complex

4. Small Team/Simple App - Learning curve not justified - REST tooling is simpler

5. Third-party Integration - Most APIs are REST - Authentication flows built for REST

docs.title: GraphQL in Simba
docs.text: Simba provides first-class GraphQL support:

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
✅ Single Request Type - No need to switch between GET/POST - All GraphQL uses POST to /graphql

✅ Schema Introspection - Auto-discover types and fields - Built-in schema explorer - Field documentation

✅ Query Builder - Visual query construction - Field selection UI - Auto-completion

✅ Variables Support - Simba variables: {{varName}} - GraphQL variables: $varName - Environment variables

✅ Test Scripts - Validate response structure - Assert on nested data - Parse GraphQL errors

✅ History & Collections - Save GraphQL queries - Organize by collection - Share with team

docs.title: 1. Create a GraphQL Request
docs.text:
Endpoint: 

https://api.github.com/graphql

Headers: 

Authorization: Bearer {{githubToken}}
Content-Type: application/json

docs.title: 2. Write a Query
docs.text:
query {
  viewer {
    login
    name
    bio
    repositories(first: 5) {
      nodes {
        name
        stargazerCount
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 3. Execute & Test
docs.text:
Test Script: 

pm.test("Status is 200", () => {
  pm.response.to.have.status(200);
});

pm.test("Response has data", () => {
  const json = pm.response.json();
  pm.expect(json.data).to.exist;
  pm.expect(json.data.viewer).to.exist;
});

pm.test("Has repositories", () => {
  const repos = pm.response.json().data.viewer.repositories.nodes;
  pm.expect(repos).to.be.an('array');
  pm.expect(repos.length).to.be.at.most(5);
});

pm.test("No GraphQL errors", () => {
  const json = pm.response.json();
  pm.expect(json.errors).to.be.undefined;
});

docs.title: Success Response
docs.text:
{
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "name": "John Doe",
      "posts": [
        { "title": "First Post" }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Error Response
docs.text:
{
  "errors": [
    {
      "message": "Cannot query field \"posst\" on type \"User\". Did you mean \"posts\"?",
      "locations": [{ "line": 4, "column": 5 }],
      "extensions": {
        "code": "GRAPHQL_VALIDATION_FAILED"
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Partial Success (GraphQL handles partial failures)
docs.text:
{
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "name": "John Doe",
      "posts": null
    }
  },
  "errors": [
    {
      "message": "Failed to fetch posts",
      "path": ["user", "posts"],
      "extensions": {
        "code": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR"
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: 1. Use Descriptive Operation Names
docs.text:
❌ Bad: 

query {
  user(id: "123") { name }
}

✅ Good: 

query GetUserProfile {
  user(id: "123") { name }
}

docs.title: 2. Use Variables for Dynamic Values
docs.text:
❌ Bad: 

query {
  user(id: "123") { name }
}

✅ Good: 

query GetUser($userId: ID!) {
  user(id: $userId) { name }
}

docs.title: 3. Request Only Needed Fields
docs.text:
❌ Bad: 

{
  user(id: "123") {
    id name email bio avatarUrl
    createdAt updatedAt status role
    preferences { theme language }
    # ... many more fields
  }
}

✅ Good: 

{
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    email
  }
}

docs.title: 4. Use Fragments for Reusability
docs.text:
❌ Bad: 

{
  user1: user(id: "1") {
    id name email
  }
  user2: user(id: "2") {
    id name email
  }
}

✅ Good: 

fragment UserInfo on User {
  id name email
}

{
  user1: user(id: "1") { ...UserInfo }
  user2: user(id: "2") { ...UserInfo }
}

docs.title: 5. Handle Errors Gracefully
docs.text:
pm.test("Check GraphQL response", () => {
  const json = pm.response.json();

  // HTTP status should be 200 even with GraphQL errors
  pm.response.to.have.status(200);

  if (json.errors) {
    console.log("GraphQL Errors:", json.errors);
    pm.expect(json.errors).to.be.empty;
  }

  pm.expect(json.data).to.exist;
});

docs.title: Pagination (Cursor-based)
docs.text:
query GetPosts($cursor: String, $limit: Int = 10) {
  posts(first: $limit, after: $cursor) {
    edges {
      node {
        id
        title
      }
      cursor
    }
    pageInfo {
      hasNextPage
      endCursor
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Pagination (Offset-based)
docs.text:
query GetUsers($offset: Int = 0, $limit: Int = 20) {
  users(offset: $offset, limit: $limit) {
    id
    name
  }
  usersCount
}

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
query SearchPosts($searchTerm: String!, $category: String) {
  posts(
    filter: {
      title_contains: $searchTerm
      category: $category
      status: PUBLISHED
    }
  ) {
    id
    title
  }
}

docs.title: Sorting
docs.text:
query GetPosts($orderBy: PostOrderByInput) {
  posts(orderBy: $orderBy) {
    id
    title
    createdAt
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "orderBy": {
    "field": "createdAt",
    "direction": "DESC"
  }
}

docs.title: Issue: "Field X doesn't exist"
docs.text:
Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Cannot query field \"posst\" on type \"User\"."
  }]
}

Solution: - Check spelling (typo: posst → posts) - Use schema explorer to see available fields - Verify field is on correct type

docs.title: Issue: Missing Required Arguments
docs.text:
Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Field \"user\" argument \"id\" of type \"ID!\" is required"
  }]
}

Solution: 

# Add missing argument
query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
  }
}

docs.title: Issue: Type Mismatch
docs.text:
Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Int cannot represent non-integer value: \"abc\""
  }]
}

Solution: 

// Use correct type
{ "limit": 10 }  // not "10"

docs.title: Issue: Nested Fields Too Deep
docs.text:
Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Query depth limit exceeded"
  }]
}

Solution: - Reduce nesting levels - Use pagination for lists - Backend has depth limits (typically 5-10 levels)

docs.title: Issue: Authentication Failed
docs.text:
Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Unauthorized",
    "extensions": { "code": "UNAUTHENTICATED" }
  }]
}

Solution: - Check authorization header - Verify token is valid - Use correct authentication method

docs.title: Complete Test Suite Example
docs.text:
// Test 1: HTTP Status
pm.test("HTTP status is 200", () => {
  pm.response.to.have.status(200);
});

// Test 2: Response time
pm.test("Response time < 500ms", () => {
  pm.expect(pm.response.responseTime).to.be.below(500);
});

// Test 3: Content-Type header
pm.test("Content-Type is JSON", () => {
  pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get("Content-Type"))
    .to.include("application/json");
});

// Test 4: No GraphQL errors
pm.test("No GraphQL errors", () => {
  const json = pm.response.json();
  if (json.errors) {
    console.error("GraphQL Errors:", json.errors);
  }
  pm.expect(json.errors).to.be.undefined;
});

// Test 5: Data structure
pm.test("Response has correct structure", () => {
  const data = pm.response.json().data;
  pm.expect(data).to.have.property('user');
  pm.expect(data.user).to.have.property('name');
  pm.expect(data.user).to.have.property('posts');
});

// Test 6: Data validation
pm.test("User data is valid", () => {
  const user = pm.response.json().data.user;
  pm.expect(user.name).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty;
  pm.expect(user.posts).to.be.an('array');
});

// Test 7: Business logic
pm.test("Has at least one post", () => {
  const posts = pm.response.json().data.user.posts;
  pm.expect(posts.length).to.be.at.least(1);
});

// Save data for next request
const userId = pm.response.json().data.user.id;
pm.environment.set("userId", userId);

docs.title: Query Complexity
docs.text:
GraphQL allows clients to request deeply nested data, which can cause performance issues:

Problematic Query: 

{
  users {          # 1000 users
    posts {        # 100 posts per user = 100,000 queries
      comments {   # 50 comments per post = 5,000,000 queries
        author {
          posts {  # N+1 problem
            comments { ... }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Solutions: 1. Pagination: Limit results 2. Depth Limiting: Backend restricts nesting 3. Complexity Analysis: Backend calculates query cost 4. DataLoader: Batch and cache requests

docs.title: Optimizing Queries
docs.text:
✅ Good Query: 

query GetDashboard {
  viewer {
    name
    recentPosts: posts(first: 5) {
      title
      commentCount  # Aggregated field
    }
  }
}

❌ Bad Query: 

query GetDashboard {
  viewer {
    name
    posts {           # All posts!
      title
      comments {      # All comments for all posts!
        text
        author {
          name
          posts {     # Recursive deep nesting
            title
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Fetching Repository Information
docs.text:
query GetRepoInfo($owner: String!, $name: String!) {
  repository(owner: $owner, name: $name) {
    name
    description
    stargazerCount
    forkCount

    primaryLanguage {
      name
      color
    }

    issues(first: 5, states: OPEN) {
      totalCount
      nodes {
        title
        createdAt
        author {
          login
        }
      }
    }

    pullRequests(first: 5, states: OPEN) {
      totalCount
      nodes {
        title
        author {
          login
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "owner": "facebook",
  "name": "react"
}

Test: 

pm.test("Repository found", () => {
  const repo = pm.response.json().data.repository;
  pm.expect(repo).to.exist;
  pm.expect(repo.name).to.equal("react");
});

pm.test("Has TypeScript as primary language", () => {
  const lang = pm.response.json().data.repository.primaryLanguage;
  pm.expect(lang.name).to.equal("TypeScript");
});

pm.test("Has stars", () => {
  const stars = pm.response.json().data.repository.stargazerCount;
  pm.expect(stars).to.be.above(1000);
});

docs.text:
Now that you understand GraphQL basics:

  1. Write Queries - Learn query syntax and patterns
  2. Use Mutations - Modify data with mutations
  3. Add Variables - Parameterize your requests
  4. Explore Schemas - Use introspection

docs.title: Related Topics
docs.text:
• REST vs GraphQL
  • Authentication - Most GraphQL APIs use Bearer tokens
  • Test Scripts - Validate GraphQL responses
  • Variables - Using Simba variables in GraphQL

docs.title: GraphQL Queries
docs.text: Master GraphQL queries to fetch exactly the data you need in a single request.

docs.title: What are GraphQL Queries?
docs.text: Queries are read operations in GraphQL (similar to GET in REST). They allow you to: - Fetch data from the server - Specify exactly which fields you want - Request nested relationships - Query multiple resources in one request

docs.title: Simple Query
docs.text:
query {
  user {
    name
    email
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "name": "John Doe",
      "email": "john@example.com"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Named Query
docs.text:
Always name your queries for better debugging and logging:

query GetUser {
  user {
    name
    email
  }
}

docs.title: Query with Arguments
docs.text:
query GetUserById {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    email
  }
}

docs.title: Scalar Fields
docs.text:
Basic data types: String, Int, Float, Boolean, ID

query {
  user(id: "123") {
    id          # ID
    name        # String
    age         # Int
    rating      # Float
    isActive    # Boolean
  }
}

docs.title: Object Fields
docs.text:
Nested objects:

query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    profile {
      bio
      website
      location
    }
  }
}

docs.title: List Fields
docs.text:
Arrays of items:

query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    posts {
      title
      content
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Nullable vs Non-Nullable
docs.text:
Schema types indicate if fields can be null:

type User {
  name: String!      # Required (non-null)
  email: String      # Optional (nullable)
  posts: [Post!]!    # Non-null array of non-null posts
}

Query: 

query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name    # Always present
    email   # Might be null
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "name": "John Doe",
      "email": null
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Single Argument
docs.text:
query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
  }
}

docs.title: Multiple Arguments
docs.text:
query {
  users(
    limit: 10
    offset: 20
    sortBy: "createdAt"
  ) {
    name
    email
  }
}

docs.title: Nested Arguments
docs.text:
Arguments can be at any level:

query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    posts(
      first: 5
      orderBy: "createdAt"
      filter: { status: "published" }
    ) {
      title
      createdAt
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Enum Arguments
docs.text:
Enums are predefined values:

query {
  posts(status: PUBLISHED, orderBy: CREATED_AT_DESC) {
    title
  }
}

docs.title: Input Object Arguments
docs.text:
Complex arguments:

query SearchPosts {
  posts(
    filter: {
      title_contains: "GraphQL"
      category: "technology"
      publishedAfter: "2024-01-01"
    }
  ) {
    title
    publishedAt
  }
}

docs.title: Aliases
docs.text: Query the same field multiple times with different arguments:

docs.title: Basic Alias
docs.text:
query {
  smallPosts: posts(limit: 3) {
    title
  }
  largePosts: posts(limit: 10) {
    title
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "smallPosts": [
      { "title": "First Post" },
      { "title": "Second Post" },
      { "title": "Third Post" }
    ],
    "largePosts": [
      { "title": "First Post" },
      // ... 7 more posts
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: Multiple Users
docs.text:
query {
  user1: user(id: "1") {
    name
  }
  user2: user(id: "2") {
    name
  }
  user3: user(id: "3") {
    name
  }
}

docs.title: Practical Example
docs.text:
query GetDashboardStats {
  todayPosts: posts(date: "2024-04-16") {
    totalCount
  }
  weekPosts: posts(dateRange: { from: "2024-04-10", to: "2024-04-16" }) {
    totalCount
  }
  monthPosts: posts(dateRange: { from: "2024-03-16", to: "2024-04-16" }) {
    totalCount
  }
}

docs.title: Fragments
docs.text: Reusable field selections.

docs.title: Defining Fragments
docs.text:
fragment UserInfo on User {
  id
  name
  email
  createdAt
}

docs.title: Using Fragments
docs.text:
query {
  user1: user(id: "1") {
    ...UserInfo
  }
  user2: user(id: "2") {
    ...UserInfo
  }
}

docs.title: Multiple Fragments
docs.text:
fragment BasicInfo on User {
  id
  name
}

fragment ContactInfo on User {
  email
  phone
}

query GetUser {
  user(id: "123") {
    ...BasicInfo
    ...ContactInfo
    posts {
      title
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Nested Fragments
docs.text:
fragment PostInfo on Post {
  title
  content
  author {
    ...UserInfo
  }
}

fragment UserInfo on User {
  name
  email
}

query {
  posts {
    ...PostInfo
  }
}

docs.title: Inline Fragments
docs.text:
For union types or interfaces:

query {
  search(text: "GraphQL") {
    ... on Post {
      title
      content
    }
    ... on User {
      name
      email
    }
    ... on Comment {
      text
      author { name }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: One Level Deep
docs.text:
query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    posts {
      title
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Multiple Levels
docs.text:
query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    posts {
      title
      comments {
        text
        author {
          name
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With Pagination
docs.text:
query GetUserFeed {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    posts(first: 10) {
      edges {
        cursor
        node {
          title
          comments(first: 5) {
            edges {
              node {
                text
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Limit/Offset (Simple)
docs.text:
query GetPosts($offset: Int = 0, $limit: Int = 10) {
  posts(offset: $offset, limit: $limit) {
    id
    title
  }
  postsCount
}

First page: 

{ "offset": 0, "limit": 10 }

Second page: 

{ "offset": 10, "limit": 10 }

docs.title: Cursor-based (Relay)
docs.text:
More robust for real-time data:

query GetPosts($cursor: String, $first: Int = 10) {
  posts(first: $first, after: $cursor) {
    edges {
      cursor
      node {
        id
        title
      }
    }
    pageInfo {
      hasNextPage
      hasPreviousPage
      startCursor
      endCursor
    }
    totalCount
  }
}

First page: 

{ "first": 10, "cursor": null }

Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "posts": {
      "edges": [
        {
          "cursor": "Y3Vyc29yOjE=",
          "node": { "id": "1", "title": "First Post" }
        }
      ],
      "pageInfo": {
        "hasNextPage": true,
        "endCursor": "Y3Vyc29yOjEw"
      }
    }
  }
}

Next page: 

{ "first": 10, "cursor": "Y3Vyc29yOjEw" }

docs.title: Backward Pagination
docs.text:
query GetPosts($cursor: String, $last: Int = 10) {
  posts(last: $last, before: $cursor) {
    edges {
      cursor
      node {
        id
        title
      }
    }
    pageInfo {
      hasPreviousPage
      startCursor
    }
  }
}

docs.title: GitHub API: Get Repository
docs.text:
query GetRepository($owner: String!, $name: String!) {
  repository(owner: $owner, name: $name) {
    name
    description
    url
    stargazerCount
    forkCount

    owner {
      login
      avatarUrl
    }

    primaryLanguage {
      name
      color
    }

    repositoryTopics(first: 5) {
      nodes {
        topic {
          name
        }
      }
    }

    defaultBranchRef {
      name
      target {
        ... on Commit {
          history(first: 1) {
            nodes {
              message
              committedDate
              author {
                name
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "owner": "facebook",
  "name": "react"
}

Test: 

pm.test("Repository found", () => {
  const repo = pm.response.json().data.repository;
  pm.expect(repo).to.exist;
  pm.expect(repo.name).to.equal("react");
});

pm.test("Has stars", () => {
  const stars = pm.response.json().data.repository.stargazerCount;
  pm.expect(stars).to.be.a('number');
  pm.expect(stars).to.be.above(100000);
});

pm.test("Has primary language", () => {
  const lang = pm.response.json().data.repository.primaryLanguage;
  pm.expect(lang).to.exist;
  pm.expect(lang.name).to.be.a('string');
});

// Save for next request
const repoUrl = pm.response.json().data.repository.url;
pm.environment.set("repoUrl", repoUrl);

docs.title: GitHub API: Get User Profile
docs.text:
query GetUserProfile {
  viewer {
    login
    name
    bio
    company
    location
    email
    avatarUrl
    websiteUrl

    repositories(
      first: 10
      orderBy: { field: STARGAZERS, direction: DESC }
    ) {
      totalCount
      nodes {
        name
        description
        stargazerCount
        url
      }
    }

    followers {
      totalCount
    }

    following {
      totalCount
    }

    contributionsCollection {
      totalCommitContributions
      totalPullRequestContributions
      totalIssueContributions
    }
  }
}

Test: 

pm.test("User authenticated", () => {
  const viewer = pm.response.json().data.viewer;
  pm.expect(viewer).to.exist;
  pm.expect(viewer.login).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty;
});

pm.test("Has repositories", () => {
  const repos = pm.response.json().data.viewer.repositories;
  pm.expect(repos.totalCount).to.be.at.least(0);
  pm.expect(repos.nodes).to.be.an('array');
});

pm.test("Contributions exist", () => {
  const contrib = pm.response.json().data.viewer.contributionsCollection;
  pm.expect(contrib.totalCommitContributions).to.be.a('number');
});

docs.title: GitHub API: Search Issues
docs.text:
query SearchIssues($query: String!, $first: Int = 10) {
  search(query: $query, type: ISSUE, first: $first) {
    issueCount
    edges {
      node {
        ... on Issue {
          title
          url
          state
          createdAt
          author {
            login
          }
          repository {
            nameWithOwner
          }
          labels(first: 5) {
            nodes {
              name
              color
            }
          }
          comments {
            totalCount
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "query": "is:open label:bug repo:facebook/react",
  "first": 5
}

docs.title: SpaceX API: Get Launches
docs.text:
query GetLaunches($limit: Int = 10) {
  launchesPast(limit: $limit) {
    mission_name
    launch_date_utc
    launch_success
    rocket {
      rocket_name
      rocket_type
    }
    launch_site {
      site_name
      site_name_long
    }
    links {
      article_link
      video_link
      mission_patch_small
    }
  }
}

Test: 

pm.test("Launches found", () => {
  const launches = pm.response.json().data.launchesPast;
  pm.expect(launches).to.be.an('array');
  pm.expect(launches.length).to.be.at.most(10);
});

pm.test("Each launch has required fields", () => {
  const launches = pm.response.json().data.launchesPast;
  launches.forEach(launch => {
    pm.expect(launch.mission_name).to.be.a('string');
    pm.expect(launch.rocket).to.exist;
    pm.expect(launch.launch_site).to.exist;
  });
});

pm.test("Has successful launches", () => {
  const launches = pm.response.json().data.launchesPast;
  const successful = launches.filter(l => l.launch_success === true);
  pm.expect(successful.length).to.be.above(0);
});

docs.title: Problem: Over-fetching
docs.text:
❌ Bad - Requesting unnecessary data: 

query {
  users {
    id name email bio avatarUrl
    createdAt updatedAt lastLoginAt
    preferences { theme language timezone }
    settings { notifications privacy }
    profile { address phone website }
  }
}

✅ Good - Only needed fields: 

query GetUserList {
  users {
    id
    name
    avatarUrl
  }
}

docs.title: Problem: N+1 Queries
docs.text:
❌ Bad - Can cause performance issues: 

query {
  posts {
    title
    author {        # Separate DB query for each post
      name
    }
    comments {
      text
      author {      # Separate DB query for each comment
        name
      }
    }
  }
}

Solution: Backend should use DataLoader or similar batching.

docs.title: Use Fragments for Consistency
docs.text:
fragment PostPreview on Post {
  id
  title
  excerpt
  publishedAt
}

query GetRecentPosts {
  recentPosts: posts(first: 5) {
    ...PostPreview
  }
}

query GetTrendingPosts {
  trendingPosts: posts(orderBy: "views", first: 5) {
    ...PostPreview
  }
}

docs.title: Limit Depth
docs.text:
# Set maximum nesting level
query GetUser {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    posts(first: 5) {        # Level 1
      title
      comments(first: 3) {   # Level 2
        text                 # Stop here - don't go deeper
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Environment: 

{
  "userId": "12345",
  "repoOwner": "facebook",
  "repoName": "react"
}

Query: 

query GetUser {
  user(id: "{{userId}}") {
    name
    email
  }
}

Resolves to: 

query GetUser {
  user(id: "12345") {
    name
    email
  }
}

docs.title: In Multiple Places
docs.text:
query GetRepository {
  repository(owner: "{{repoOwner}}", name: "{{repoName}}") {
    name
    url
    issues(first: 5, labels: ["{{issueLabel}}"]) {
      nodes {
        title
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With GraphQL Variables (Recommended)
docs.text:
Better approach - use GraphQL variables:

Query: 

query GetUser($userId: ID!) {
  user(id: $userId) {
    name
    email
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "userId": "{{userId}}"
}

This way, Simba variables are resolved in the variables JSON, not in the query itself.

docs.title: Complete Test Suite
docs.text:
// 1. Status check
pm.test("HTTP 200", () => {
  pm.response.to.have.status(200);
});

// 2. No GraphQL errors
pm.test("No GraphQL errors", () => {
  const json = pm.response.json();
  if (json.errors) {
    console.error("GraphQL Errors:", JSON.stringify(json.errors, null, 2));
  }
  pm.expect(json.errors).to.be.undefined;
});

// 3. Response structure
pm.test("Has data object", () => {
  const json = pm.response.json();
  pm.expect(json).to.have.property('data');
  pm.expect(json.data).to.be.an('object');
});

// 4. Expected data exists
pm.test("User data exists", () => {
  const user = pm.response.json().data.user;
  pm.expect(user).to.exist;
});

// 5. Field validation
pm.test("User has required fields", () => {
  const user = pm.response.json().data.user;
  pm.expect(user).to.have.property('id');
  pm.expect(user).to.have.property('name');
  pm.expect(user).to.have.property('email');
});

// 6. Type validation
pm.test("Fields have correct types", () => {
  const user = pm.response.json().data.user;
  pm.expect(user.id).to.be.a('string');
  pm.expect(user.name).to.be.a('string');
  pm.expect(user.posts).to.be.an('array');
});

// 7. Business logic
pm.test("Name is not empty", () => {
  const user = pm.response.json().data.user;
  pm.expect(user.name).to.have.length.above(0);
});

// 8. Nested data
pm.test("Posts have titles", () => {
  const posts = pm.response.json().data.user.posts;
  posts.forEach(post => {
    pm.expect(post).to.have.property('title');
    pm.expect(post.title).to.be.a('string');
  });
});

// 9. Performance
pm.test("Response < 1s", () => {
  pm.expect(pm.response.responseTime).to.be.below(1000);
});

// 10. Save data for next request
const userId = pm.response.json().data.user.id;
pm.environment.set("lastUserId", userId);

docs.title: Testing Pagination
docs.text:
pm.test("Pagination works", () => {
  const result = pm.response.json().data.posts;

  // Has pageInfo
  pm.expect(result).to.have.property('pageInfo');
  pm.expect(result.pageInfo).to.have.property('hasNextPage');

  // Has edges
  pm.expect(result.edges).to.be.an('array');

  // Each edge has cursor and node
  result.edges.forEach(edge => {
    pm.expect(edge).to.have.property('cursor');
    pm.expect(edge).to.have.property('node');
  });

  // Save cursor for next page
  if (result.pageInfo.hasNextPage) {
    pm.environment.set("nextCursor", result.pageInfo.endCursor);
  }
});

docs.title: Testing Error Handling
docs.text:
// Check for partial errors
pm.test("Handle partial data", () => {
  const json = pm.response.json();

  if (json.errors) {
    // Some data may still be present
    console.warn("Partial errors:", json.errors);

    // Check which fields failed
    json.errors.forEach(error => {
      console.log("Failed path:", error.path);
      console.log("Error message:", error.message);
    });
  }

  // Data might still be useful
  if (json.data) {
    pm.expect(json.data).to.be.an('object');
  }
});

docs.title: Issue: Fields Not Returned
docs.text:
Problem: 

query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    posts {
      title
    }
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "name": "John",
      "posts": null
    }
  }
}

Solutions: 1. Field might be nullable (check schema) 2. User has no posts (empty array vs null) 3. Permission issue (not authorized to see posts) 4. Try adding error checking:

query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    postsCount          # Try count first
    posts(first: 1) {   # Limit to test
      title
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Issue: Query Too Complex
docs.text:
Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Query is too complex",
    "extensions": {
      "code": "COMPLEXITY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    }
  }]
}

Solution: - Reduce nesting depth - Use pagination (first: 10) - Split into multiple queries - Remove unnecessary fields

docs.title: Issue: Field Doesn't Exist
docs.text:
Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Cannot query field \"posst\" on type \"User\". Did you mean \"posts\"?"
  }]
}

Solution: - Use schema explorer to see available fields - Check spelling - Field might be on different type

docs.title: Issue: Variables Not Working
docs.text:
Query: 

query GetUser($userId: ID!) {
  user(id: $userId) {
    name
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "userId": 123  # ❌ Wrong type (should be string)
}

Solution: 

{
  "userId": "123"  # ✅ Correct
}

docs.title: Best Practices Checklist
docs.text:
✅ Always name your queries 

query GetUserProfile { ... }

✅ Use variables for dynamic values 

query GetUser($id: ID!) { ... }

✅ Request only needed fields 

{ user { name } }  # Not: { user { id name email bio ... } }

✅ Use fragments for reusability 

fragment UserInfo on User { ... }

✅ Add pagination to lists 

posts(first: 10) { ... }

✅ Handle errors in tests 

if (json.errors) { console.error(json.errors); }

✅ Limit nesting depth 

# Max 3-4 levels deep

✅ Use aliases for multiple queries 

user1: user(id: "1") { ... }
user2: user(id: "2") { ... }

docs.text:

docs.title: GraphQL Schema Explorer
docs.text: Discover and explore GraphQL APIs using introspection and schema documentation.

docs.title: What is Introspection?
docs.text:
Introspection is a powerful GraphQL feature that allows you to query the API schema itself. You can discover: - Available types - Fields on each type - Arguments for fields - Documentation and descriptions - Deprecated fields - Directives

This is like having interactive API documentation built into the API itself.

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text: ✅ Self-Documentation - No separate docs needed ✅ Always Up-to-Date - Schema reflects current API ✅ Type Discovery - See all available types ✅ Field Discovery - Find fields you can query ✅ Auto-completion - Build queries faster ✅ Validation - Catch errors before sending

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Learning New APIs - Explore structure
  • Building Queries - Find available fields
  • Code Generation - Generate types/interfaces
  • Documentation - Auto-generate docs
  • Testing - Validate schema changes

docs.title: Opening Schema Explorer
docs.text:
1. Create a new GraphQL request
  2. Enter the GraphQL endpoint URL
  3. Add authentication (if needed)
  4. Click "Schema Explorer" button
  5. Simba automatically runs introspection query

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Type Browser - Browse all types
  • Field Inspector - See fields, arguments, types
  • Documentation - Read inline descriptions
  • Search - Find types by name
  • Deprecation Warnings - See deprecated fields
  • Nested Exploration - Click types to explore

docs.title: Basic Introspection
docs.text:
This is what Simba runs automatically:

query IntrospectionQuery {
  __schema {
    queryType {
      name
    }
    mutationType {
      name
    }
    subscriptionType {
      name
    }
    types {
      ...FullType
    }
    directives {
      name
      description
      locations
      args {
        ...InputValue
      }
    }
  }
}

fragment FullType on __Type {
  kind
  name
  description
  fields(includeDeprecated: true) {
    name
    description
    args {
      ...InputValue
    }
    type {
      ...TypeRef
    }
    isDeprecated
    deprecationReason
  }
  inputFields {
    ...InputValue
  }
  interfaces {
    ...TypeRef
  }
  enumValues(includeDeprecated: true) {
    name
    description
    isDeprecated
    deprecationReason
  }
  possibleTypes {
    ...TypeRef
  }
}

fragment InputValue on __InputValue {
  name
  description
  type {
    ...TypeRef
  }
  defaultValue
}

fragment TypeRef on __Type {
  kind
  name
  ofType {
    kind
    name
    ofType {
      kind
      name
      ofType {
        kind
        name
        ofType {
          kind
          name
          ofType {
            kind
            name
            ofType {
              kind
              name
              ofType {
                kind
                name
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Simplified Introspection
docs.text:
For exploring manually:

{
  __schema {
    types {
      name
      kind
      description
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Query Root Type
docs.text:
Find all available queries:

{
  __schema {
    queryType {
      name
      fields {
        name
        description
        args {
          name
          type {
            name
            kind
          }
        }
        type {
          name
          kind
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Example Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "__schema": {
      "queryType": {
        "name": "Query",
        "fields": [
          {
            "name": "user",
            "description": "Get user by ID",
            "args": [
              {
                "name": "id",
                "type": {
                  "name": null,
                  "kind": "NON_NULL"
                }
              }
            ],
            "type": {
              "name": "User",
              "kind": "OBJECT"
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Mutation Root Type
docs.text:
Find all available mutations:

{
  __schema {
    mutationType {
      name
      fields {
        name
        description
        args {
          name
          type {
            name
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Specific Type Details
docs.text:
Get details about a specific type:

{
  __type(name: "User") {
    name
    kind
    description
    fields {
      name
      description
      type {
        name
        kind
      }
    }
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "__type": {
      "name": "User",
      "kind": "OBJECT",
      "description": "A user in the system",
      "fields": [
        {
          "name": "id",
          "description": "Unique identifier",
          "type": {
            "name": null,
            "kind": "NON_NULL"
          }
        },
        {
          "name": "name",
          "description": "User's full name",
          "type": {
            "name": "String",
            "kind": "SCALAR"
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Enum Values
docs.text:
Explore enum options:

{
  __type(name: "PostStatus") {
    name
    enumValues {
      name
      description
      isDeprecated
      deprecationReason
    }
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "__type": {
      "name": "PostStatus",
      "enumValues": [
        {
          "name": "DRAFT",
          "description": "Post is in draft mode",
          "isDeprecated": false,
          "deprecationReason": null
        },
        {
          "name": "PUBLISHED",
          "description": "Post is published",
          "isDeprecated": false,
          "deprecationReason": null
        },
        {
          "name": "ARCHIVED",
          "description": "Post is archived",
          "isDeprecated": false,
          "deprecationReason": null
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Input Types
docs.text:
See input object fields:

{
  __type(name: "CreateUserInput") {
    name
    kind
    inputFields {
      name
      description
      type {
        name
        kind
      }
      defaultValue
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Type Kinds
docs.text:
GraphQL has several type kinds:

Kind Description Example SCALAR Basic data types String, Int, Float, Boolean, ID OBJECT Complex types with fields User, Post, Comment INTERFACE Abstract type Node, Timestamped UNION One of several types SearchResult (Post | User) ENUM Set of values PostStatus, Role INPUT_OBJECT Input for mutations CreateUserInput LIST Array of types [User], [String!] NON_NULL Required type String!, ID!

docs.title: Type Wrappers
docs.text:
Types can be wrapped to indicate nullability and lists:

# Nullable scalar
String

# Required scalar
String!

# Nullable list of nullable strings
[String]

# Required list of nullable strings
[String]!

# Nullable list of required strings
[String!]

# Required list of required strings
[String!]!

Understanding the syntax:

String          # Can be null: null, "hello"
String!         # Cannot be null: "hello"
[String]        # Can be null or list: null, [], ["a", null]
[String]!       # Cannot be null but list can have nulls: [], ["a", null]
[String!]       # Can be null but list items can't: null, [], ["a", "b"]
[String!]!      # Nothing can be null: [], ["a", "b"]

docs.title: GitHub API Schema Exploration
docs.text:
Explore Repository type:

{
  __type(name: "Repository") {
    name
    description
    fields {
      name
      description
      type {
        name
        kind
      }
      args {
        name
        description
        type {
          name
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Find all mutations:

{
  __schema {
    mutationType {
      fields {
        name
        description
      }
    }
  }
}

Explore specific mutation:

{
  __type(name: "Mutation") {
    fields(includeDeprecated: false) {
      name
      description
      args {
        name
        description
        type {
          name
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Step 1: Find Root Queries
docs.text:
{
  __schema {
    queryType {
      fields {
        name
        description
      }
    }
  }
}

Result: Available queries like user, users, repository, etc.

docs.title: Step 2: Explore Query Arguments
docs.text:
{
  __type(name: "Query") {
    fields {
      name
      args {
        name
        type {
          name
          kind
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Example: user(id: ID!) requires an ID argument.

docs.title: Step 3: Explore Return Type
docs.text:
{
  __type(name: "User") {
    fields {
      name
      type {
        name
        kind
      }
    }
  }
}

Result: User has fields: id, name, email, posts, etc.

docs.title: Step 4: Build Query
docs.text:
query GetUser {
  user(id: "123") {
    id
    name
    email
    posts {
      title
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Reading Descriptions
docs.text:
GraphQL schemas can include descriptions:

"""
Represents a user in the system.
Users can create posts and comments.
"""
type User {
  """
  Unique identifier for the user.
  This ID is stable and never changes.
  """
  id: ID!

  """
  The user's full name.
  Maximum length: 100 characters.
  """
  name: String!

  """
  User's email address.
  Must be unique and valid.
  """
  email: String!
}

Introspection captures these:

{
  __type(name: "User") {
    description
    fields {
      name
      description
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Deprecation
docs.text:
type User {
  id: ID!
  fullName: String!
  name: String! @deprecated(reason: "Use fullName instead")
}

Find deprecated fields:

{
  __type(name: "User") {
    fields(includeDeprecated: true) {
      name
      isDeprecated
      deprecationReason
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Detecting Changes
docs.text:
Run introspection before and after updates:

// Save current schema
const schema = pm.response.json().data.__schema;
pm.collectionVariables.set('oldSchema', JSON.stringify(schema));

// Compare later
const oldSchema = JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('oldSchema'));
const newSchema = pm.response.json().data.__schema;

// Check for removed types
const oldTypes = oldSchema.types.map(t => t.name);
const newTypes = newSchema.types.map(t => t.name);
const removedTypes = oldTypes.filter(t => !newTypes.includes(t));

if (removedTypes.length > 0) {
  console.log('Removed types:', removedTypes);
}

docs.title: Breaking Changes
docs.text:
pm.test("No breaking changes", () => {
  const oldSchema = JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('oldSchema'));
  const newSchema = pm.response.json().data.__schema;

  // Check if Query type still exists
  pm.expect(newSchema.queryType).to.exist;

  // Check critical fields
  const oldQueryFields = oldSchema.queryType.fields.map(f => f.name);
  const newQueryFields = newSchema.queryType.fields.map(f => f.name);

  oldQueryFields.forEach(field => {
    pm.expect(newQueryFields).to.include(field, 
      `Query.${field} was removed!`);
  });
});

docs.title: Custom Directives
docs.text:
{
  __schema {
    directives {
      name
      description
      locations
      args {
        name
        type {
          name
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Common directives: - @deprecated - Mark fields as deprecated - @include(if: Boolean!) - Include field conditionally - @skip(if: Boolean!) - Skip field conditionally - @specifiedBy - Link to scalar specification

docs.title: Interfaces
docs.text:
{
  __type(name: "Node") {
    name
    kind
    description
    possibleTypes {
      name
    }
  }
}

Result: 

{
  "data": {
    "__type": {
      "name": "Node",
      "kind": "INTERFACE",
      "description": "An object with an ID",
      "possibleTypes": [
        { "name": "User" },
        { "name": "Post" },
        { "name": "Comment" }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Unions
docs.text:
{
  __type(name: "SearchResult") {
    name
    kind
    possibleTypes {
      name
      description
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Test Schema Health
docs.text:
pm.test("Schema is valid", () => {
  const schema = pm.response.json().data.__schema;

  // Has query type
  pm.expect(schema.queryType).to.exist;
  pm.expect(schema.queryType.name).to.equal('Query');

  // Has types
  pm.expect(schema.types).to.be.an('array');
  pm.expect(schema.types.length).to.be.above(0);

  // Has standard scalars
  const typeNames = schema.types.map(t => t.name);
  ['String', 'Int', 'Float', 'Boolean', 'ID'].forEach(scalar => {
    pm.expect(typeNames).to.include(scalar);
  });
});

docs.title: Test Required Fields
docs.text:
pm.test("User type has required fields", () => {
  const schema = pm.response.json().data.__schema;
  const userType = schema.types.find(t => t.name === 'User');

  pm.expect(userType).to.exist;

  const fieldNames = userType.fields.map(f => f.name);
  ['id', 'name', 'email'].forEach(field => {
    pm.expect(fieldNames).to.include(field);
  });
});

docs.title: Disabling Introspection
docs.text:
Some production APIs disable introspection for security:

{
  __schema {
    types {
      name
    }
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "GraphQL introspection is not allowed, but the query contained __schema or __type"
  }]
}

Workarounds: - Use development/staging endpoint - Request schema file from API team - Use public documentation - Contact API support

docs.title: Generate TypeScript Types
docs.text:
Use schema to generate types:

// Generated from schema
type User = {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string | null;
  posts: Post[];
};

type Post = {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  content: string | null;
  author: User;
};

docs.title: Generate GraphQL Fragments
docs.text:
# Auto-generate from User type
fragment UserFields on User {
  id
  name
  email
  createdAt
}

docs.title: Generate Documentation
docs.text:
Create API docs from schema:

# User

A user in the system.

## Fields

- `id: ID!` - Unique identifier
- `name: String!` - User's full name
- `email: String` - User's email (nullable)
- `posts: [Post!]!` - User's posts

docs.title: 1. Explore Schema First
docs.text: Before writing queries: 1. Run introspection 2. Browse available types 3. Check field arguments 4. Read descriptions

docs.title: 2. Check Deprecations
docs.text:
{
  __type(name: "User") {
    fields(includeDeprecated: true) {
      name
      isDeprecated
      deprecationReason
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 3. Understand Type Relationships
docs.text:
Map out object relationships:

User
  ├─ posts: [Post!]!
  │   └─ comments: [Comment!]!
  │       └─ author: User!
  └─ followers: [User!]!

docs.title: 4. Save Common Fragments
docs.text:
# Save as collection variable
fragment UserPreview on User {
  id
  name
  avatarUrl
}

docs.title: 5. Version Control Schema
docs.text:
// Export schema to file
const schema = pm.response.json().data.__schema;
const timestamp = new Date().toISOString();
const filename = `schema-${timestamp}.json`;

// In actual use, save to version control
console.log('Schema snapshot:', JSON.stringify(schema, null, 2));

docs.title: Issue: Introspection Disabled
docs.text:
Error: "GraphQL introspection is not allowed"

Solutions: 1. Use development endpoint 2. Request schema file 3. Use different environment 4. Contact API support

docs.title: Issue: Large Schema Response
docs.text:
Problem: Schema introspection times out or returns huge response.

Solution: Query specific types:

# Instead of full schema
{
  __type(name: "Query") {
    fields {
      name
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Issue: Type Not Found
docs.text:
{
  __type(name: "Usr") {  # Typo
    name
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "data": {
    "__type": null
  }
}

Solution: Fix typo or list all types first:

{
  __schema {
    types {
      name
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Schema Explorer Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Action Shortcut Search types Ctrl/Cmd + F Expand type → or Enter Collapse type ← or Backspace Navigate ↑ ↓ Go to definition Ctrl/Cmd + Click Copy type name Ctrl/Cmd + C

docs.title: Good Schema Design
docs.text:
"""
A blog post created by a user.
Posts can have multiple comments and tags.
"""
type Post {
  """
  Unique identifier.
  Format: UUID v4
  """
  id: ID!

  """
  Post title.
  Max length: 200 characters
  Required for all posts
  """
  title: String!

  """
  Post content in Markdown format.
  Can be empty for draft posts.
  """
  content: String

  """
  Publication timestamp.
  Null for draft posts.
  """
  publishedAt: DateTime

  """
  Post author.
  Never null - posts always have an author.
  """
  author: User!

  """
  Post tags for categorization.
  Empty array for untagged posts.
  """
  tags: [Tag!]!
}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Queries - Build queries using schema
  • Mutations - Discover available mutations
  • Variables - Use correct types from schema
  • Tutorial - Complete workflow

docs.title: Related Topics
docs.text:
• GraphQL Overview
  • Test Scripts
  • Collection Organization
  • API Documentation

docs.title: GraphQL Variables
docs.text: Master GraphQL variables to write reusable, dynamic queries and mutations.

docs.title: What are GraphQL Variables?
docs.text: Variables make GraphQL operations dynamic and reusable. Instead of hard-coding values in your queries, you define variables that can be passed separately.

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text: ✅ Reusability - Same query with different inputs ✅ Type Safety - Variables are strongly typed ✅ Security - Prevent injection attacks ✅ Clean Code - Separate data from query logic ✅ DRY Principle - Don't repeat values

docs.title: Without Variables (Bad)
docs.text:
query {
  user(id: "123") {
    name
    posts(limit: 10) {
      title
    }
  }
}

Problems: - Hard to reuse with different IDs - Values embedded in query string - Must regenerate entire query to change values

docs.title: With Variables (Good)
docs.text:
Query: 

query GetUser($userId: ID!, $postLimit: Int) {
  user(id: $userId) {
    name
    posts(limit: $postLimit) {
      title
    }
  }
}

Variables (JSON): 

{
  "userId": "123",
  "postLimit": 10
}

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
query OperationName($variableName: Type) {
  # Use variable
}

Components: - $variableName - Variable name (starts with $) - Type - GraphQL type (String, Int, ID, etc.) - ! - Required (non-null)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Required variable
$userId: ID!

# Optional variable
$limit: Int

# Optional with default
$limit: Int = 10

# Required array
$userIds: [ID!]!

# Optional array of optional strings
$tags: [String]

# Input object
$input: CreateUserInput!

docs.title: Scalar Types
docs.text:
query Examples(
  $id: ID!,                # Unique identifier
  $name: String!,          # Text
  $age: Int,               # Integer
  $price: Float,           # Decimal
  $isActive: Boolean       # true/false
) {
  # ...
}

Variables: 

{
  "id": "abc123",
  "name": "John Doe",
  "age": 30,
  "price": 19.99,
  "isActive": true
}

docs.title: List Types
docs.text:
query SearchUsers(
  $ids: [ID!]!,            # Required array of required IDs
  $tags: [String],         # Optional array of optional strings
  $scores: [Int!]          # Optional array of required integers
) {
  # ...
}

Variables: 

{
  "ids": ["1", "2", "3"],
  "tags": ["admin", null, "editor"],
  "scores": [85, 90, 78]
}

docs.title: Enum Types
docs.text:
query GetPosts(
  $status: PostStatus!,      # DRAFT, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED
  $orderBy: SortOrder        # ASC, DESC
) {
  posts(status: $status, orderBy: $orderBy) {
    title
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "status": "PUBLISHED",
  "orderBy": "DESC"
}

docs.title: Input Object Types
docs.text:
mutation CreateUser($input: CreateUserInput!) {
  createUser(input: $input) {
    id
    name
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "input": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "profile": {
      "bio": "Software developer",
      "website": "https://johndoe.com"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Query-level Defaults
docs.text:
query GetPosts(
  $limit: Int = 10,
  $offset: Int = 0,
  $status: PostStatus = PUBLISHED
) {
  posts(limit: $limit, offset: $offset, status: $status) {
    title
  }
}

Variables (empty): 

{}
Uses defaults: limit: 10, offset: 0, status: PUBLISHED

Variables (partial): 

{
  "limit": 5
}
Uses: limit: 5, offset: 0 (default), status: PUBLISHED (default)

docs.title: When to Use Defaults
docs.text:
✅ Good cases: - Common values (pagination limits) - Reasonable fallbacks - Optional filters

❌ Avoid for: - Security-critical values - Business logic - User-specific data

docs.title: Using Simba Variables
docs.text: Simba has its own variable system ({{varName}}) that works alongside GraphQL variables.

docs.title: Simba Variables in GraphQL Variables
docs.text:
Recommended approach:

Environment/Collection Variables: 

{
  "userId": "12345",
  "repoOwner": "facebook",
  "repoName": "react"
}

GraphQL Query: 

query GetRepository($owner: String!, $name: String!) {
  repository(owner: $owner, name: $name) {
    name
    stargazerCount
  }
}

GraphQL Variables (using Simba variables): 

{
  "owner": "{{repoOwner}}",
  "name": "{{repoName}}"
}

Flow: 1. Simba resolves {{repoOwner}} → "facebook" 2. Simba resolves {{repoName}} → "react" 3. GraphQL receives: 

{
  "owner": "facebook",
  "name": "react"
}

docs.title: Simba Variables Directly in Query (Not Recommended)
docs.text:
query GetUser {
  user(id: "{{userId}}") {
    name
  }
}

Why avoid this: - Loses GraphQL type safety - Can't reuse query with different values easily - Mixing two variable systems

docs.title: Pre-request Script Generation
docs.text:
// Generate dynamic values
const timestamp = Date.now();
const randomId = Math.floor(Math.random() * 1000);

// Set as Simba variables
pm.variables.set('timestamp', timestamp);
pm.variables.set('randomId', randomId);

// Set entire GraphQL variables object
const graphqlVars = {
  input: {
    name: `User ${randomId}`,
    email: `user${timestamp}@example.com`
  }
};
pm.variables.set('graphqlVariables', JSON.stringify(graphqlVars));

Use in Variables panel: 

{
  "input": {
    "name": "User {{randomId}}",
    "email": "user{{timestamp}}@example.com"
  }
}

docs.title: Nested Input Objects
docs.text:
mutation CreateBlogPost($input: CreatePostInput!) {
  createPost(input: $input) {
    id
    title
    author { name }
    tags { name }
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "input": {
    "title": "GraphQL Variables Guide",
    "content": "Learn how to use GraphQL variables...",
    "author": {
      "connect": {
        "id": "user123"
      }
    },
    "tags": {
      "create": [
        { "name": "graphql" },
        { "name": "tutorial" }
      ]
    },
    "metadata": {
      "featured": true,
      "publishedAt": "2024-04-16T10:00:00Z"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Multiple Operations with Shared Variables
docs.text:
# Define both query and mutation
query GetUser($userId: ID!) {
  user(id: $userId) {
    name
    email
  }
}

mutation UpdateUser($userId: ID!, $input: UpdateUserInput!) {
  updateUser(id: $userId, input: $input) {
    name
    updatedAt
  }
}

To execute specific operation: 

query GetUser($userId: ID!) {
  user(id: $userId) {
    name
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "userId": "123"
}

docs.title: Filter Objects
docs.text:
query SearchPosts($filter: PostFilterInput!) {
  posts(filter: $filter) {
    edges {
      node {
        title
        publishedAt
      }
    }
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "filter": {
    "AND": [
      {
        "title": {
          "contains": "GraphQL"
        }
      },
      {
        "publishedAt": {
          "gte": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
        }
      },
      {
        "OR": [
          {
            "category": {
              "equals": "tutorial"
            }
          },
          {
            "tags": {
              "some": {
                "name": {
                  "in": ["graphql", "api"]
                }
              }
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: GitHub API: Create Repository
docs.text:
mutation CreateRepository(
  $name: String!,
  $description: String,
  $visibility: RepositoryVisibility!,
  $hasIssues: Boolean = true
) {
  createRepository(input: {
    name: $name
    description: $description
    visibility: $visibility
    hasIssuesEnabled: $hasIssues
  }) {
    repository {
      id
      name
      url
    }
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "name": "my-awesome-project",
  "description": "A test repository",
  "visibility": "PUBLIC"
}

Test: 

pm.test("Repository created with correct name", () => {
  const repo = pm.response.json().data.createRepository.repository;
  pm.expect(repo.name).to.equal(pm.variables.get('repoName'));
});

// Save for cleanup
pm.environment.set('lastRepoId', repo.id);

docs.title: GitHub API: Paginated Query
docs.text:
query GetRepositories(
  $owner: String!,
  $first: Int = 10,
  $after: String
) {
  user(login: $owner) {
    repositories(
      first: $first,
      after: $after,
      orderBy: { field: UPDATED_AT, direction: DESC }
    ) {
      edges {
        cursor
        node {
          name
          stargazerCount
        }
      }
      pageInfo {
        hasNextPage
        endCursor
      }
    }
  }
}

First page: 

{
  "owner": "torvalds",
  "first": 5
}

Next page: 

// In test script
const pageInfo = pm.response.json().data.user.repositories.pageInfo;
if (pageInfo.hasNextPage) {
  pm.environment.set('nextCursor', pageInfo.endCursor);
}

Second request: 

{
  "owner": "torvalds",
  "first": 5,
  "after": "{{nextCursor}}"
}

docs.title: GitHub API: Search with Filters
docs.text:
query SearchRepositories(
  $query: String!,
  $first: Int = 10,
  $type: SearchType = REPOSITORY
) {
  search(query: $query, type: $type, first: $first) {
    repositoryCount
    edges {
      node {
        ... on Repository {
          name
          description
          stargazerCount
          primaryLanguage {
            name
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "query": "language:javascript stars:>1000 fork:false",
  "first": 20
}

docs.title: Test Variable Types
docs.text:
pm.test("Variables have correct types", () => {
  const variables = JSON.parse(pm.request.body.graphql.variables || '{}');

  // Check types
  pm.expect(variables.userId).to.be.a('string');
  pm.expect(variables.limit).to.be.a('number');
  pm.expect(variables.isActive).to.be.a('boolean');
  pm.expect(variables.tags).to.be.an('array');
});

docs.title: Test Variable Resolution
docs.text:
pm.test("Simba variables resolved", () => {
  const variables = JSON.parse(pm.request.body.graphql.variables || '{}');

  // Should not contain {{}} after resolution
  const jsonString = JSON.stringify(variables);
  pm.expect(jsonString).to.not.include('{{');
  pm.expect(jsonString).to.not.include('}}');
});

docs.title: Test Required Variables
docs.text:
// Pre-request: Validate required variables
const requiredVars = ['userId', 'email'];
const missing = [];

requiredVars.forEach(varName => {
  const value = pm.variables.get(varName);
  if (!value) {
    missing.push(varName);
  }
});

if (missing.length > 0) {
  throw new Error(`Missing required variables: ${missing.join(', ')}`);
}

docs.title: Dynamic Variable Generation
docs.text:
// Pre-request script
const now = new Date();

// Date filtering
pm.variables.set('startDate', new Date(now.setDate(now.getDate() - 7)).toISOString());
pm.variables.set('endDate', new Date().toISOString());

// Random data
pm.variables.set('randomInt', Math.floor(Math.random() * 100));
pm.variables.set('uuid', pm.variables.replaceIn('{{$guid}}'));

// Computed values
const limit = pm.variables.get('pageSize') || 10;
const offset = (pm.variables.get('currentPage') || 0) * limit;
pm.variables.set('offset', offset);

docs.title: Pattern 1: Pagination State
docs.text:
// Initialize pagination
pm.collectionVariables.set('currentPage', 0);
pm.collectionVariables.set('pageSize', 10);

// Calculate offset
const page = pm.collectionVariables.get('currentPage');
const size = pm.collectionVariables.get('pageSize');
pm.variables.set('offset', page * size);

Query: 

query GetItems($offset: Int!, $limit: Int!) {
  items(offset: $offset, limit: $limit) {
    id
    name
  }
  itemsCount
}

Next page button logic (test script): 

// Increment page
const currentPage = pm.collectionVariables.get('currentPage');
pm.collectionVariables.set('currentPage', currentPage + 1);

docs.title: Pattern 2: Filter Builder
docs.text:
// Build filter from environment
const filters = {};

const status = pm.environment.get('filterStatus');
if (status) filters.status = status;

const category = pm.environment.get('filterCategory');
if (category) filters.category = category;

const minPrice = pm.environment.get('filterMinPrice');
if (minPrice) filters.price = { gte: parseFloat(minPrice) };

pm.variables.set('filters', JSON.stringify(filters));

Query: 

query SearchProducts($filters: ProductFilterInput) {
  products(filter: $filters) {
    id
    name
    price
  }
}

Variables: 

{
  "filters": {{filters}}
}

docs.title: Pattern 3: Batch Operations
docs.text:
// Generate multiple items
const items = [];
for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
  items.push({
    name: `Item ${i + 1}`,
    value: Math.random() * 100
  });
}

pm.variables.set('batchItems', JSON.stringify(items));

Mutation: 

mutation CreateItems($items: [CreateItemInput!]!) {
  createItems(inputs: $items) {
    count
    items {
      id
      name
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Missing Required Variables
docs.text:
Query: 

query GetUser($userId: ID!) {
  user(id: $userId) {
    name
  }
}

Variables (empty): 

{}

Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Variable \"$userId\" of required type \"ID!\" was not provided."
  }]
}

docs.title: Type Mismatch
docs.text:
Variables: 

{
  "userId": 123  // Should be string
}

Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Variable \"$userId\" got invalid value 123; Expected type ID; ID cannot represent a non-string value: 123"
  }]
}

Fix: 

{
  "userId": "123"
}

docs.title: Invalid Input Object
docs.text:
Variables: 

{
  "input": {
    "name": "John"
    // Missing required field "email"
  }
}

Error: 

{
  "errors": [{
    "message": "Variable \"$input\" got invalid value { name: \"John\" }; Field value.email of required type String! was not provided."
  }]
}

docs.title: 1. Always Use Variables for Dynamic Values
docs.text:
❌ Bad: 

query {
  user(id: "123") { name }
}

✅ Good: 

query GetUser($userId: ID!) {
  user(id: $userId) { name }
}

docs.title: 2. Use Descriptive Variable Names
docs.text:
❌ Bad: 

query GetPosts($n: Int, $s: String) {
  posts(limit: $n, sortBy: $s) { title }
}

✅ Good: 

query GetPosts($limit: Int, $sortBy: String) {
  posts(limit: $limit, sortBy: $sortBy) { title }
}

docs.title: 3. Set Sensible Defaults
docs.text:
query GetPosts(
  $limit: Int = 10,      # Default page size
  $offset: Int = 0,      # Start at beginning
  $sortBy: String = "createdAt",
  $sortOrder: SortOrder = DESC
) {
  posts(limit: $limit, offset: $offset, sortBy: $sortBy, order: $sortOrder) {
    title
  }
}

docs.title: 4. Group Related Variables
docs.text:
❌ Bad: 

mutation CreateUser(
  $name: String!,
  $email: String!,
  $bio: String,
  $website: String
) {
  # ...
}

✅ Good: 

mutation CreateUser($input: CreateUserInput!) {
  # All fields in input object
}

docs.title: 5. Validate Before Sending
docs.text:
// Pre-request validation
const email = pm.variables.get('email');
const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;

if (!emailRegex.test(email)) {
  throw new Error(`Invalid email format: ${email}`);
}

docs.title: Issue: Variable Not Defined
docs.text:
Query uses variable but not defined: 

query GetUser {
  user(id: $userId) { name }  # ❌ $userId not defined
}

Fix: 

query GetUser($userId: ID!) {  # ✅ Define variable
  user(id: $userId) { name }
}

docs.title: Issue: Simba Variable Not Resolving
docs.text:
Variables: 

{
  "userId": "{{userId}}"
}

After execution still shows {{userId}}:

Causes: 1. Variable not set in environment/collection 2. Typo in variable name 3. Variable set in wrong scope

Debug: 

// Pre-request
console.log('userId =', pm.variables.get('userId'));
console.log('All variables:', pm.variables.toObject());

docs.title: Issue: JSON Syntax Error in Variables
docs.text:
Invalid JSON: 

{
  "name": "John",  # ❌ Comments not allowed
  tags: ['a', 'b']  # ❌ Keys must be quoted, use double quotes
}

Fix: 

{
  "name": "John",
  "tags": ["a", "b"]
}

docs.title: Variable Cheat Sheet
docs.text: Type Definition Example Value String $name: String! "John Doe" Int $age: Int 30 Float $price: Float 19.99 Boolean $active: Boolean true ID $id: ID! "abc123" Enum $status: Status! "ACTIVE" List $ids: [ID!]! ["1", "2"] Input $input: UserInput! { "name": "..." } Optional $name: String null or "value" Default $limit: Int = 10 Uses 10 if not provided

docs.text:

docs.title: DELETE Requests
docs.text: Learn how to delete resources using the DELETE HTTP method in Simba.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
DELETE is an HTTP method used to remove resources from a server.

Key Characteristics: - �� Removes a resource permanently - ⚠️ Usually irreversible - ✅ Idempotent (repeating has same effect) - 📝 May return deleted data or confirmation

docs.title: Simple Resource Deletion
docs.text:
Example: Delete a user

Method: DELETE
URL: https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/1

Headers:
  Authorization: Bearer {{authToken}}

Expected Responses:

200 OK (with deleted resource): 

{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Leanne Graham",
  "email": "test@example.com",
  "deleted": true
}

204 No Content (successful, no body): 

(Empty response)

Test Script: 

pm.test("Delete successful", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 204]);
});

pm.test("Response time acceptable", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.time).to.be.below(500);
});

// If 200 response with body
if (pm.response.code === 200) {
    pm.test("Deleted resource returned", () => {
        const data = pm.response.json();
        pm.expect(data).to.have.property('id');
    });
}

docs.title: Delete Specific Resource
docs.text:
Method: DELETE
URL: https://api.example.com/posts/{{postId}}

Pre-request Script: 

// Get ID from environment
const postId = pm.environment.get('postId');

if (!postId) {
    throw new Error('postId not set in environment');
}

console.log(`Deleting post ID: ${postId}`);

Test Script: 

pm.test("Post deleted", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.equal(200);
});

pm.test("Deleted post ID matches", () => {
    const data = pm.response.json();
    const expectedId = parseInt(pm.environment.get('postId'));
    pm.expect(data.id).to.equal(expectedId);
});

// Clear deleted ID from environment
pm.environment.unset('postId');

docs.title: Delete with Filters
docs.text:
Example: Delete all posts by user

Method: DELETE
URL: https://api.example.com/posts?userId=1&permanent=true

Headers:
  Authorization: Bearer {{authToken}}

Query Parameters: 

userId: 1
permanent: true
confirm: yes

Test Script: 

pm.test("Bulk delete successful", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.equal(200);
});

pm.test("Delete count returned", () => {
    const result = pm.response.json();
    pm.expect(result).to.have.property('deletedCount');
    pm.expect(result.deletedCount).to.be.above(0);

    console.log(`Deleted ${result.deletedCount} posts`);
});

docs.title: Soft Delete (Logical Deletion)
docs.text:
Resource marked as deleted but remains in database:

Method: DELETE (or PATCH)
URL: https://api.example.com/users/1

Response:
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "John Doe",
  "deleted": true,
  "deletedAt": "2026-04-16T10:30:00Z"
}

Benefits: - ✅ Can be restored - ✅ Maintains referential integrity - ✅ Audit trail preserved

docs.title: Hard Delete (Physical Deletion)
docs.text:
Resource permanently removed from database:

Method: DELETE
URL: https://api.example.com/users/1?permanent=true

Response:
204 No Content

Characteristics: - ⚠️ Cannot be undone - 🔒 Better for privacy/GDPR compliance - 💾 Frees storage space

docs.title: Delete with Request Body
docs.text:
Some APIs accept DELETE with body (non-standard but used):

Method: DELETE
URL: https://api.example.com/posts/bulk

Headers:
  Content-Type: application/json

Body:
{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
  "reason": "Spam content",
  "confirmedBy": "admin@example.com"
}

Test Script: 

pm.test("Bulk delete successful", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.equal(200);
});

pm.test("All items deleted", () => {
    const result = pm.response.json();
    const requestIds = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];

    pm.expect(result.deletedIds).to.be.an('array');
    pm.expect(result.deletedIds.length).to.equal(requestIds.length);

    requestIds.forEach(id => {
        pm.expect(result.deletedIds).to.include(id);
    });
});

docs.title: Delete with Confirmation
docs.text:
Require confirmation token:

Method: DELETE
URL: https://api.example.com/accounts/{{accountId}}

Headers:
  Authorization: Bearer {{authToken}}
  X-Confirm-Token: {{confirmToken}}

Pre-request Script (get confirmation token): 

// Request confirmation token first
pm.sendRequest({
    url: pm.environment.get('baseUrl') + '/accounts/confirm-delete',
    method: 'POST',
    header: {
        'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + pm.environment.get('authToken'),
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: {
        mode: 'raw',
        raw: JSON.stringify({
            accountId: pm.environment.get('accountId'),
            password: pm.environment.get('password')
        })
    }
}, (err, res) => {
    if (!err && res.code === 200) {
        const confirmToken = res.json().token;
        pm.environment.set('confirmToken', confirmToken);
        console.log('✅ Confirmation token obtained');
    } else {
        console.log('❌ Failed to get confirmation token');
    }
});

docs.title: Delete with ETag
docs.text:
Prevent deleting modified resource:

Method: DELETE
URL: https://api.example.com/posts/1

Headers:
  If-Match: "686897696a7c876b7e"

Test Script: 

pm.test("Delete or conflict", () => {
    const status = pm.response.code;

    if (status === 200 || status === 204) {
        console.log("✅ Resource deleted successfully");
    } else if (status === 412) {
        console.log("⚠️  Resource was modified, cannot delete");
    } else {
        pm.expect.fail(`Unexpected status: ${status}`);
    }
});

docs.title: Pattern 1: Verify Before Delete
docs.text:
// 1. GET resource to verify it exists
pm.sendRequest(pm.environment.get('baseUrl') + '/users/1', (err, res) => {
    if (res.code === 200) {
        const user = res.json();
        console.log(`Deleting user: ${user.name}`);
        pm.environment.set('userToDelete', JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
        console.log('User not found, skipping delete');
    }
});

// 2. DELETE request (in next request)
// 3. Verify deletion (GET should return 404)

docs.title: Pattern 2: Delete with Backup
docs.text:
Pre-request Script: 

// Backup resource before deleting
const resourceId = pm.environment.get('resourceId');

pm.sendRequest({
    url: `${pm.environment.get('baseUrl')}/posts/${resourceId}`,
    method: 'GET'
}, (err, res) => {
    if (!err && res.code === 200) {
        // Save backup
        pm.collectionVariables.set('deletedBackup', JSON.stringify(res.json()));
        console.log('✅ Backup saved');
    }
});

docs.title: Pattern 3: Cascade Delete
docs.text:
Delete parent and all children:

Method: DELETE
URL: https://api.example.com/collections/{{collectionId}}?cascade=true

Test Script: 

pm.test("Cascade delete successful", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.equal(200);
});

pm.test("All related resources deleted", () => {
    const result = pm.response.json();

    pm.expect(result).to.have.property('collection');
    pm.expect(result).to.have.property('deletedRequests');
    pm.expect(result).to.have.property('deletedFolders');

    console.log(`Deleted collection and ${result.deletedRequests} requests`);
});

docs.title: Common Status Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning Action 200 OK Deleted (with body) ✅ Success 204 No Content Deleted (no body) ✅ Success 202 Accepted Delete queued ⏳ Check status later 400 Bad Request Invalid request ❌ Check parameters 401 Unauthorized Not authenticated ❌ Check auth token 403 Forbidden No permission ❌ Check permissions 404 Not Found Resource doesn't exist ⚠️ Already deleted or never existed 409 Conflict Cannot delete (has dependencies) ❌ Delete dependencies first 410 Gone Already deleted ⚠️ Resource permanently removed

docs.title: Test for Errors
docs.text:
pm.test("Delete request status", () => {
    const status = pm.response.code;

    switch(status) {
        case 200:
        case 204:
            console.log("✅ Delete successful");
            break;
        case 404:
            console.log("⚠️  Resource not found");
            break;
        case 409:
            const error = pm.response.json();
            console.log("❌ Cannot delete:", error.message);
            break;
        case 403:
            console.log("❌ Permission denied");
            break;
        default:
            pm.expect.fail(`Unexpected status: ${status}`);
    }
});

pm.test("Error response has details", () => {
    if (pm.response.code >= 400) {
        const error = pm.response.json();
        pm.expect(error).to.have.property('message');
    }
});

docs.title: Verify Resource is Gone
docs.text:
After DELETE, verify with GET:

// In DELETE test script
pm.test("Delete successful", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200, 204]);
});

// Save resource ID for verification
const deletedId = pm.environment.get('resourceId');
pm.collectionVariables.set('deletedResourceId', deletedId);

// Create follow-up GET request to verify 404

Verification GET Request: 

Method: GET
URL: https://api.example.com/posts/{{deletedResourceId}}

Test Script: 

pm.test("Resource no longer exists", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([404, 410]);
});

pm.test("Error message indicates deletion", () => {
    const error = pm.response.json();
    pm.expect(error.message).to.match(/not found|deleted|gone/i);
});

// Clean up
pm.collectionVariables.unset('deletedResourceId');

docs.title: Test Cascade Deletion
docs.text:
pm.test("Parent deleted", () => {
    const result = pm.response.json();
    pm.expect(result.collection.deleted).to.be.true;
});

pm.test("Children deleted", () => {
    const result = pm.response.json();
    pm.expect(result.deletedRequests).to.be.above(0);
    console.log(`${result.deletedRequests} child resources deleted`);
});

// Verify child is gone
const childId = pm.collectionVariables.get('childResourceId');
pm.sendRequest({
    url: `${pm.environment.get('baseUrl')}/requests/${childId}`,
    method: 'GET'
}, (err, res) => {
    pm.test("Child resource deleted", () => {
        pm.expect(res.code).to.equal(404);
    });
});

docs.title: 1. Confirmation Required
docs.text:
// Pre-request script
const confirmDelete = pm.collectionVariables.get('confirmDelete');

if (confirmDelete !== 'YES') {
    throw new Error('Delete not confirmed! Set confirmDelete="YES" to proceed.');
}

console.log('⚠️  Proceeding with deletion...');

docs.title: 2. Dry Run Mode
docs.text:
Method: DELETE
URL: https://api.example.com/posts/1?dryRun=true

Response shows what would be deleted: 

{
  "dryRun": true,
  "wouldDelete": {
    "post": 1,
    "comments": 15,
    "likes": 42
  },
  "warning": "This is a dry run. No data was actually deleted."
}

docs.title: 3. Trash/Recycle Bin Pattern
docs.text:
// Soft delete first
pm.test("Moved to trash", () => {
    const result = pm.response.json();
    pm.expect(result.status).to.equal('trashed');
    pm.expect(result.trashedAt).to.exist;

    // Can be restored within 30 days
    console.log('Resource moved to trash, restoreable until:', 
                result.permanentDeleteAt);
});

docs.title: ✅ DO
docs.text:
Verify authentication: 

pm.test("Authenticated", () => {
    const token = pm.environment.get('authToken');
    pm.expect(token).to.exist;
});

Check permissions before delete: 

// Ensure user has delete permission
const userRole = pm.environment.get('userRole');
if (userRole !== 'admin' && userRole !== 'owner') {
    throw new Error('Insufficient permissions to delete');
}

Log deletions: 

const deleted = pm.response.json();
console.log('Deleted:', {
    id: deleted.id,
    type: deleted.type,
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
});

// Save to deletion history
const history = JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('deleteHistory') || '[]');
history.push({
    id: deleted.id,
    deletedAt: new Date().toISOString()
});
pm.collectionVariables.set('deleteHistory', JSON.stringify(history));

Handle idempotency: 

pm.test("Delete is idempotent", () => {
    const status = pm.response.code;

    // Both success cases
    if (status === 404 || status === 410) {
        console.log('Resource already deleted (idempotent)');
    } else {
        pm.expect(status).to.be.oneOf([200, 204]);
    }
});

docs.title: ❌ DON'T
docs.text:
Don't delete without verification: 

// ❌ Bad
DELETE /users/{{userId}}

// ✅ Good - verify first
if (pm.environment.get('userId') === '1') {
    throw new Error('Cannot delete system admin');
}

Don't ignore delete failures: 

// ❌ Bad - only check success
pm.test("Status 200", () => {
    pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.equal(200);
});

// ✅ Good - handle all cases
pm.test("Delete handled correctly", () => {
    const status = pm.response.code;

    if (status === 409) {
        console.log('Cannot delete - has dependencies');
    } else if (status === 403) {
        console.log('Permission denied');
    } else {
        pm.expect(status).to.be.oneOf([200, 204]);
    }
});

docs.title: Complete Delete Workflow Example
docs.text: Collection: Delete User Account

docs.title: 1. Request: Verify User Exists
docs.text: GET /users/{{userId}}

docs.title: 2. Request: Check Dependencies
docs.text:
GET /users/{{userId}}/dependencies

Test: Ensure no active subscriptions, pending orders, etc.

docs.title: 3. Request: Soft Delete (Move to Trash)
docs.text:
DELETE /users/{{userId}}

Test: User status = trashed

docs.title: 4. Request: Verify Soft Delete
docs.text:
GET /users/{{userId}}

Test: Status code 200, but user.deleted = true

docs.title: 5. Request: Hard Delete (After Confirmation)
docs.text:
DELETE /users/{{userId}}?permanent=true

Headers:
  X-Confirm-Token: {{confirmToken}}

Test: Status 204, resource permanently removed

docs.title: 6. Request: Verify Hard Delete
docs.text:
GET /users/{{userId}}

Test: Status 404 or 410
